mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 2% in the subsequent month.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of industrial production and housing starts captures the balance between supply capacity and consumer demand. When INDPRO expands while HOUST contracts, it indicates supply bottlenecks outpacing demand, which compresses corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression of forward returns, or the signal direction flips consistently across different market volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: In a persistent inflation environment, rising CPIAUCSL readings force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than markets price in. This delayed policy response creates a valuation multiple contraction that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases do not correlate with subsequent multiple compression, or equity returns remain positive despite sustained inflation readings above 3.5% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in residential investment (RSAFS) directly reflects consumer confidence and credit availability. Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS increase near-term liquidity and spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward returns drops below 0.10 over a 3-year rolling window, or negative RSAFS prints do not precede market declines.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the industrial production index below 105 signals a contraction in manufacturing output that precedes broader economic slowdowns. This supply-side deceleration reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 105 fail to predict equity drawdowns, or forward returns remain positive despite sustained manufacturing contraction.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) captures labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth accelerates while UNRATE remains below 4.0%, it indicates wage-push inflation pressures that trigger multiple compression and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline unemployment-only model in predicting forward returns, or equity multiples expand despite tight labor conditions.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-peak rate cycle, housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator for consumer spending and construction-related earnings. Rising HOUST readings in this specific regime signal economic resilience and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases do not precede equity gains during rate peak periods, or the signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations.
